strict_win_deltas.delta: P2 splits the feature into single-purpose functions (readLedgerEvents, validateEvent, readPayoutRules, calculatePayout) with one centralized try/catch dispatch in main(), while P1 crams parsing, validation, dedup, computation, and output into a single ~140-line runPayout function that manually repeats a payoutError(...); return; pattern at roughly ten call sites.

P1's `rules = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(rulesPath, 'utf8'));` line has no try/catch, so a missing or malformed data/payout-rules.json crashes the process with an uncaught exception (stack trace to stderr, wrong exit code) instead of exiting 2 with a JSON error, whereas P2 routes that same read through `readJsonFile`, which throws a PayoutError caught by main()'s catch block to emit `{"error":"unreadable_payout_rules"}`/`{"error":"invalid_payout_rules"}` with exit code 2.

P2's new tests submit the identical charge-2 event twice in the success case and add a dedicated conflicting-duplicate test asserting the exact `{error:'conflicting_duplicate', id}` payload, directly exercising the idempotent/conflicting-duplicate handling that is the task's headline requirement, while P1's two new tests never repeat an event id and so never exercise that behavior at all.

P1 imports and reuses Node's built-in `require('util').isDeepStrictEqual` for duplicate-content comparison, while P2 hand-rolls an 8-line recursive `canonicalJson` key-sorting serializer to achieve the same equality check, adding custom logic that reimplements standard-library functionality.
